mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning and liquidity withdrawals, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the UNRATE threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and residential housing starts indicates synchronized real-economy weakness that compresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. The signal activates only when both series decline simultaneously.
failure_condition: The combined indicator's Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not diverge significantly between co-declining and divergent INDPRO/HOUST regimes (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.5% year-over-year, rising real consumer credit balances reflect debt-fueled consumption that sustains corporate revenues, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when RSAFS rises during CPI > 3.5% periods, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when inflation expectations decouple from headline CPI.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects sustained manufacturing output growth that directly supports corporate revenue streams, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days regardless of monetary conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference between rising and falling INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, the cost of capital exceeds the historical equilibrium for corporate debt servicing, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 55% of instances following the FEDFUNDS threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of nonfarm payrolls and growth in real consumer credit balances signals a self-reinforcing labor-income consumption loop that boosts corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. The mechanism requires both series to rise concurrently.
failure_condition: The joint indicator's predictive power collapses when payroll growth outpaces credit expansion, or forward returns fail to differentiate between synchronized growth and divergent labor-credit regimes (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid GDP growth exceeding 3.0% annualized, rising housing starts act as a leading indicator for durable goods demand and construction-related corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when HOUST rises during high-GDP growth periods, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
